steps.title: Spot the qualifier that undercuts the finding
steps.content: The passage reports that supplementation appeared unaffected, then immediately adds: “however, supplementation was rare, highly varied, and poorly tracked.” This “however” is the decisive signal—poor data quality means the conclusion cannot be taken as definitive.

steps.title: Infer what the unreliable data logically permits us to claim
steps.content:
When data is unreliable, you cannot rule out alternative possibilities.

The poor supplementation tracking means you cannot exclude the possibility that supplementation would actually benefit chimpanzees, particularly during autumn when vitamin D is naturally lower.

The other three either assert unsupported differences (A), contradict the data (C), or reverse the causal relationship (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
